overview.minides: More premium than the Bolero — the Neo facelift adds LED DRLs, 9-inch touchscreen, alloy wheels, cruise control, and leatherette seats to its proven body-on-frame DNA.
overview.description: The Mahindra Bolero Neo facelift was launched on 6 October 2025 with prices from ₹8.49 lakh (ex-showroom). It is offered in five variants — N4, N8, N10(R), N11, and N10 Opt — all powered by the 1.5-litre mHawk100 3-cylinder turbo-diesel producing 99 bhp and 260 Nm, mated to a 5-speed manual with rear-wheel drive. The 2025 facelift brings a new horizontal-slat chrome grille, new 16-inch dark metallic grey alloy wheels, dual-tone colour options (Jeans Blue DT, Pearl White DT), two new interior themes (Mocha Brown, Lunar Grey), leatherette upholstery, a 9-inch touchscreen on N10 and above, Frequency Dependent Dampers (FDD), LED DRLs, a rear-view camera, steering-mounted controls, and USB Type-C charging. The N10(O)/N10 Opt gets a braking differential lock for low-traction situations. The N11 adds a dual-tone exterior pack with 16-inch alloys and body cladding. All variants seat 7 in a 5+2 layout with side-facing third-row seats. The Bolero Neo is a rebadged successor to the TUV300.

sharedSpecs.fddNote: Frequency Dependent Dampers for improved ride comfort — new for 2025 facelift
